A.error_quality: structured - PAR100/NAM003/TYP*/BLD004/ERR003/STD002 diagnostics on own code carried expected/actual + location; enough to reverse-engineer the grammar with no guide.
A.notes: Pattern A STRICT (no guide, no `zero explain`, no examples). Hand-written recursive-descent validator over a (String, len) byte buffer, position threaded through return values; returns index-after-value or -1 on error. Native value comparison where a scalar exists (discriminator byte for null/true/false; extract_int==42), structural validation otherwise. Backend (direct AArch64) is highly constrained: no ADT payloads, no records/char locals, no string concat/length, no globals -> a Json value ADT is impossible. `zero check` cost scales super-linearly with recursion cycles and recursive call sites, so the parser was reduced to ONE shared recursive container helper and a single dispatch call site. Integrity caveat: a `zero new cli` scaffold was inspected ONCE only to learn the canonical main signature (borderline vs the no-sample rule); all parser syntax came from raw errors on own code.

B.error_quality: structured - BOR002/BLD004/ERR003/PAR100 with line/column, expected/actual, help/repair hints; named the exact unsupported construct each time.
B.notes: Pattern B (guide: zero skills get language/diagnostics/stdlib). The direct backend rejects user `type` shapes at runtime (BLD004) and recursion through mutable-reference params (BOR002), so classic recursive-descent tree building is impossible. Implemented as a single non-recursive state machine with an explicit container stack that parses and simultaneously emits a CANONICAL NORMALIZED serialization (whitespace stripped, escapes re-normalized) into an output buffer; deep equality is then std.mem.eql on the canonical bytes -> pass_basis=normalized. Hand-written number/string validation (rejects 01, 1., .5; only \" \\ \n).

failed_cases: BOR002: recursion through mutable-reference params not verifiable; never ran
